data.title: Tether offers $1B bid to acquire Juventus FC, aiming to purchase controlling stake and remaining shares, which Exor rejects.
data.description: Tether has expressed its intention to acquire a majority stake in Juventus, along with the remaining shares, in an attempt to take control of the football club. The offer made by Tether to purchase Exor's stake has been rejected by Exor.

data.title: Fobi AI announces partial revocation order and non-brokered private placement, excluding distribution in the US.
data.description: This news information is not intended to be shared with U.S. news wire services or disseminated within the United States. Please refrain from distributing this to such channels within the country. Thank you for your attention to this matter.

data.title: In 2025, Ohio State's Caleb Downs wins the Paycom Jim Thorpe Award for top college football defensive back. Presented on ESPN.
data.description: Caleb Downs from Ohio State University has been awarded the 2025 Paycom Jim Thorpe Award, recognizing him as the top defensive back in college football. The announcement was made during The Home Depot College Football Awards on ESPN, where Downs was honored for his outstanding performance, athletic ability, and character. Downs leads the No. 1 defense in the country, and received the award on behalf of the Oklahoma Sports Hall of Fame, Jim Thorpe Association, and presenting entity.

data.title: Nasdaq-100 Index® undergoes annual reconstitution effective December 22, 2025; changes to be implemented before market opens.
data.description: On December 12, 2025, Nasdaq revealed the outcomes of the yearly reorganization of the Nasdaq-100 Index® (NDX®). The changes will take effect before the market opens on December 22, 2025. Stay tuned for the latest updates on Nasdaq (Nasdaq: NDAQ).

data.title: FDA approves Milestone's self-administered nasal spray CARDAMYST™ for PSVT in adults, benefiting over 2 million Americans.
data.description: Montreal-based Milestone ® Pharmaceuticals Inc. and Charlotte, N.C. reported on Dec. 12, 2025, that the U.S. FDA granted approval for their first commercial product, CARDAMYST™ (etripamil) nasal spray. This prescription medication is designed for the conversion of acute symptomatic episodes of paroxysmal supraventricular tachycardia (PSVT) to sinus rhythm in adults. More than two million Americans with PSVT will benefit from this rapid-acting self-administered treatment option that can be used outside of traditional healthcare settings. CARDAMYST is set to be accessible in retail pharmacies by the first quarter of 2026. Milestone ® Pharmaceuticals Inc. is listed on Nasdaq as MIST.

data.title: Doug Keck awarded 2025 Mother Angelica Award by EWTN Global Catholic Network for his decades of service in media.
data.description: Irondale, Alabama, December 12, 2025 - The esteemed EWTN Global Catholic Network has awarded Doug Keck, a veteran broadcaster and esteemed media executive, with the prestigious 2025 Mother Angelica Award. This honor, the Network's most distinguished recognition, celebrates Keck's years of dedication and commitment to the industry.

data.title: SpaceX, led by Elon Musk, worth $800 billion ahead of IPO. Insider shares selling at $421 each. It could be most valuable private company globally.
data.description: With the sale of insider shares at $421 each, Mr. Musk's rocket company is poised to become the world's most valuable private company. The company is preparing for a potential initial public offering in the coming year.

data.title: Sverica Capital Management sells Coastal to TCS for $700M, a 5-year partnership that quadrupled its size.
data.description: In Austin, Texas, Sverica Capital Management LP disclosed the sale of Coastal Cloud Holdings, LLC to Tata Consultancy Services Limited for a hefty sum of $700 million. Through a fruitful five-year partnership, Coastal underwent remarkable growth, expanding by over four times its original size. This growth was accompanied by sustained high profitability, positioning Coastal as a respected market leader in its industry.

data.title: Dow Jones rises after Fed rate cut, Microsoft stock down; Palantir jumps. Indexes aim for record highs.
data.description: The Dow Jones index saw a boost on Wednesday following a rate cut by the Federal Reserve. Microsoft's stock experienced a decline amidst these developments. The market is looking towards record highs after the Fed rate cut and Chairman Powell's remarks. Additionally, Palantir experienced a significant increase in its stock value. This live coverage provides insights into the latest stock market movements and events.

data.title: LangSmith boosts debugging for intricate AI agents with new tools, such as AI assistant Polly and LangSmith Fetch CLI to aid LLM app development.
data.description: LangSmith has unveiled new debugging tools tailored for deep agents, such as AI assistant Polly and LangSmith Fetch CLI, to improve LLM application development. These tools are designed to streamline the debugging process and make it more efficient for developers working with deep agents. This announcement marks a significant advancement in the realm of LLM development, providing developers with advanced resources to enhance their work.

data.title: Fed cuts rates by 0.25% to 3.5%–3.75%, revealing internal divide. 3 members dissent. New patience stance for 2026 signaled.
data.description: The Federal Reserve has reduced interest rates by 25 basis points to a range of 3.5%–3.75%, marking the third consecutive cut and highlighting a growing divide among policymakers. While meeting market expectations, the decision was made despite lingering high inflation, with a weaker labor market cited as the primary reason. Of note, during the December meeting, there was notable dissent within the Fed, with three members advocating for different actions. Governor Stephen Miran called for a larger 50-basis-point reduction, while Kansas City Fed President Jeffrey Schmid and Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee supported maintaining the rates. The updated guidance from the Federal Open Market Committee signals a more cautious approach for 2026, with a focus on assessing incoming data comprehensively before making any further changes. For more details, the complete article is accessible on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Nanjing Massacre in 1937 saw over 300,000 Chinese killed by Japanese aggressors, sparking mourning and remembrance.
data.description: In December 13, 1937, during the occupation of Nanjing in Jiangsu Province, East China, by Japanese forces, the Nanjing Massacre occurred, leading to the brutal killing of more than 300,000 Chinese individuals. This heinous act is recorded as a grave crime...

data.title: EverMind launches EverMemOS, an open-source Memory OS to tackle challenges in AI memory. Dec.12, 2025.
data.description: On December 12, 2025, based in San Mateo, California, EverMind, an AI infrastructure company, unveiled EverMemOS, an open-source Memory Operating System. This new system aims to tackle a significant hurdle in artificial intelligence by providing machines with scalable, long-term memory. This development is crucial as it could revolutionize AI capabilities and pave the way for more advanced technologies.

data.title: Fed cuts key rate to stem job losses, S&P 500 reacts to 2026 projections. Chair Powell's news conference.
data.description: The Federal Reserve has reduced its key interest rate in an effort to address job losses, with the S&P 500 responding to projections for 2026 and remarks made by Chair Jerome Powell during a news conference. This move by the Fed comes as part of efforts to stabilize the economy amid challenging circumstances. Following this decision, the S&P 500 has shown an upward trend, reflecting positive market sentiment towards the measures taken by the Federal Reserve.

data.title: Discover latest updates on BNB Chain: key metrics, new projects, and ecosystem development in weekly report. Click for details.
data.description: Discover the most recent updates regarding the BNB Chain in the weekly ecosystem report. The report covers essential figures, such as key metrics, new initiatives, and notable ecosystem enhancements. Stay informed about the latest developments shaping the BNB Chain's ecosystem.

data.title: Fed cuts rates for the third time, revealing internal divisions. Uncertainty ahead with borrowing costs expected to see limited decrease next year.
data.description: The central bank's choice to decrease interest rates for the third consecutive meeting sparked debate due to internal disagreements. This is expected to restrict the extent to which borrowing costs will decrease in the upcoming year.

data.title: Wall Street rallies despite Powell's neutral stance; Dow jumps 558 points to 48,120, Russell 2000 surges 1.7%. Fed holds rates at 3.50–3.75%.
data.description: Stocks surged despite Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell maintaining a neutral stance following the bank's third rate cut, dispelling hopes for further easing. The Russell 2000 rose 1.7%, reaching all-time highs, while the Dow Jones gained 558 points to 48,120, and the S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 also climbed. Although a rate cut in January was ruled out, investors were encouraged by the Fed's improved economic outlook, projecting stronger growth, softer inflation, and steady interest rates. The Federal Open Market Committee reduced the federal funds rate to 3.50–3.75%, citing labor and inflation conditions. Powell indicated rates are "within a broad range of estimates of neutral," implying a pause to assess future developments.

data.title: Introducing LangSmith Fetch: CLI tool for seamless agent debugging, integrating LangSmith tracing, optimizing workflows.
data.description: LangChain has recently launched LangSmith Fetch, a command-line interface (CLI) tool that seamlessly incorporates LangSmith tracing into developers' workflows straight from the terminal. This innovative tool streamlines agent debugging processes, enhancing efficiency and productivity. With LangSmith Fetch, developers can now easily access and leverage LangSmith tracing directly within their existing workflows, revolutionizing the debugging experience. This advancement is set to revolutionize the developer community, with a significant emphasis on optimizing agent debugging activities.

data.title: Federal Reserve cuts interest rate by 25 basis points, benefiting Bitcoin and crypto market. Past rates show mixed reactions.
data.description: The Federal Reserve has reduced its benchmark interest rate by 25 basis points, injecting fresh liquidity into a volatile market. This move is seen as positive for cryptocurrencies like Bitcoin, as it historically leads to easier monetary conditions. However, reactions are not always immediate or straightforward. Rate cuts stimulate liquidity by lowering borrowing costs and yields on low-risk assets, driving investors towards higher-risk markets such as crypto. While Bitcoin has historically benefited from rate cuts, reactions can vary, with past instances showing short-term declines before a clearer direction emerges. The initial response to rate cuts can involve volatility, pullbacks, or consolidation before a consistent trend is established. In some cases, traders may already have priced in the rate cut, leading to opposite market movements as participants adjust positions.

data.title: Fed rate cut boosts investor sentiment; Cipher Mining stock near highs. Russell 2000 index surged 1% to 2,550 points. Dow Jones, S&P 500, Nasdaq 100 gained slightly. Small caps seen as biggest beneficiaries of lower borrowing costs.
data.description: Investor sentiment received a boost on Wednesday after the Federal Reserve made its third consecutive cut in interest rates. This led to gains in high-beta stocks related to clean energy, crypto mining, and space tech. Cipher Mining's stock is trading near recent highs, sparking curiosity about its future performance. The Russell 2000 index, represented by the iShares Russell 2000 ETF (NYSE: IWM), surged to 2,550 points, achieving fresh record levels with a nearly 1% increase during the session. Small-cap stocks are being viewed favorably by investors due to the decline in borrowing costs. The Dow Jones, S&P 500, and Nasdaq 100 also experienced marginal gains. More details are available on Benzinga.com.
